What We Do

Five Programmes.
One Mission.

Each programme is designed to address a specific barrier preventing children from thriving in Mbale City.

📚
Programme 01

Education & Life Skills

Bridging the gap between school enrolment and genuine learning through tutoring, mentorship, and digital literacy training for children in informal settlements.

Learn More →
⚽
Programme 02

Sports & Talent Development

Using football, basketball, and athletics as tools for discipline, teamwork, and identifying exceptional youth talent for regional and national competitions.

Learn More →
🛡️
Programme 03

Child Protection

Running safe space programmes, child-friendly reporting mechanisms, and community-based child safeguarding networks across six neighbourhoods.

Learn More →
💜
Programme 04

Girls Empowerment

Tackling gender barriers through menstrual health support, STEM clubs, entrepreneurship training, and leadership circles for adolescent girls.

Learn More →
🌱
Programme 05

Community Sustainability

Equipping families with small-business skills, savings groups, kitchen gardens, and environmental education to build economic resilience.

Learn More →
Global Goals

Aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals

Our programmes directly contribute to four of the UN's 17 Global Goals, ensuring every action we take is connected to the wider global agenda for human flourishing.

SDG 4: Quality Education

Ensuring inclusive, equitable quality education for every child we serve.

SDG 5: Gender Equality

Empowering girls and women through targeted programmes and safe spaces.

SDG 13: Climate Action

Integrating environmental literacy and sustainable agriculture into community work.

SDG 16: Peace, Justice & Strong Institutions

Building child-safe communities grounded in accountability and transparency.

Our Full SDG Commitment →

Who We Are

About Hope for the
Next Generation

A grassroots organisation born out of a conviction that every child in Mbale City deserves the opportunity to discover their potential and contribute to their community.

2020
Year Founded
Started with 12 children,
a football, and a belief
that sport changes lives.
2,400+
Children Reached
6
Communities
Our Story

Born from the Streets of Mbale

Hope for the Next Generation was founded in 2020 by Ivan Wangwe after years of watching children in Mbale City's informal settlements fall through the cracks of the education system — talented, curious, full of potential, but with no outlet and no support.

Starting with a single football pitch and twelve children, we proved that sport could be a gateway to education, discipline, and community belonging. Within two years, that experiment had grown into five distinct programmes serving over 2,400 children across six communities.

Today, HNG operates at the intersection of sport, education, child protection, gender equality, and environmental sustainability — always with the child at the centre.

See Our Impact →
👁️

Our Vision

"A Mbale City where every child — regardless of background, gender, or circumstance — grows up safe, educated, and empowered to lead."

🎯

Our Mission

"To restore hope and unlock potential among vulnerable children and youth in Mbale City through grassroots sports, quality education, talent development, and sustainable livelihoods."

What Guides Us

Our Core Values

❤️

Child-Centred

Every decision starts with the child's best interest at its core.

🎯

Results-Driven

We measure impact rigorously and adapt programmes based on evidence.

🛡️

Safeguarding First

Zero tolerance for any harm; robust protection policies and training.

👥

Community-Led

Local knowledge and ownership drive every initiative we run.

👁️

Transparent

Open books, published reports, and accountable governance structures.

🌱

Sustainable

Building local capacity so communities thrive long after our support.

Stories of Change

Behind Every Number, a Child

Girls Empowerment
"Before HNG, I thought school wasn't for me. Now I'm the top science student in my class and I want to be a doctor. The STEM club changed everything."
A
Amina, 14

Amina joined our STEM club after dropping out in Primary 5. With a menstrual health kit, tutoring support, and a female mentor, she returned to school and now regularly tops her class.

Sports & Talent
"Football gave me focus. I used to spend my days on the street with nothing to do. Coach found me, trained me, and now I represent the region."
D
Derrick, 16

Derrick was identified in a community tournament at age 13. Three years of structured coaching later, he plays for the Eastern Uganda Under-17 squad and holds a sports bursary.

Community Sustainability
"Our savings group helped us buy seeds and start a kitchen garden. We no longer worry about whether our children will eat. That peace of mind is everything."
S
Sarah's Family

Sarah joined a village savings group in 2022. With a micro-grant and business training, her family now grows surplus vegetables and sells at the local market, generating steady income.

Help Us Write More Stories Like These

Your support directly funds the programmes behind these outcomes.

Make an Impact →
Global Goals

Our SDG Commitment

Hope for the Next Generation directly contributes to four of the UN's 17 Sustainable Development Goals. Our work is deliberately designed to advance the 2030 Agenda.

Why the SDGs Matter to Us

The UN Sustainable Development Goals aren't an abstract framework for us — they're a mirror held up to the work we do every day. When a girl stays in school because she has a menstrual health kit, that's SDG 5. When a family plants a kitchen garden instead of going hungry, that's SDG 13.

We chose to formally align with SDGs 4, 5, 13, and 16 because our monitoring data shows our programmes generate the most measurable impact in these four goal areas.

SDG 4
Quality Education

"Ensure inclusive and equitable quality education and promote lifelong learning opportunities for all."

Linked Programmes
Education & Life SkillsGirls Empowerment

Our Connection

Education is the spine of our work. Through tutoring centres, digital literacy, bursaries, and mentorship, we ensure children in Mbale's informal settlements don't just attend school — they actually learn.

Our Results

1,200+ children in tutoring85% grade-level improvement42 secondary school bursaries3 digital learning hubs

Key Targets

✓ 4.1 Free, equitable quality primary and secondary education
✓ 4.4 Relevant skills for employment
✓ 4.5 Eliminate gender disparities in education

SDG 5
Gender Equality

"Achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls."

Linked Programmes
Girls EmpowermentEducation & Life Skills

Our Connection

Our Girls Empowerment programme directly tackles the structural barriers keeping girls out of school and leadership. From period poverty to STEM exclusion, we address each barrier with targeted, dignity-first interventions.

Our Results

400+ girls reached annually92% school retention rate60 micro-grants awarded2 STEM clubs with female mentors

Key Targets

✓ 5.1 End discrimination against women and girls
✓ 5.5 Ensure women's full participation in leadership
✓ 5.6 Universal access to reproductive health

SDG 13
Climate Action

"Take urgent action to combat climate change and its impacts."

Linked Programmes
Community Sustainability

Our Connection

Our Community Sustainability programme embeds environmental literacy in everything we do. Kitchen gardens, tree-planting, and eco-education teach families that ecological resilience and economic resilience are the same thing.

Our Results

95 kitchen gardens established500+ trees planted in 20246 communities with eco-education40 families in sustainable agriculture training

Key Targets

✓ 13.1 Strengthen resilience to climate hazards
✓ 13.3 Education and awareness on climate mitigation
✓ 13.b Promote local capacity building

SDG 16
Peace, Justice & Strong Institutions

"Promote peaceful and inclusive societies for sustainable development."

Linked Programmes
Child ProtectionCommunity Sustainability

Our Connection

Our Child Protection programme builds community safeguarding infrastructure — trained volunteers, child-friendly safe spaces, and transparent referral pathways — that hold communities accountable for every child's safety.

Our Results

120 safeguarding volunteers trained6 child-friendly safe spacesZero unresolved cases in 202495% caregiver satisfaction rating

Key Targets

✓ 16.1 Reduce violence against children
✓ 16.2 End abuse, exploitation, and trafficking of children
✓ 16.7 Inclusive, participatory decision-making

Get Involved

Whether you give, volunteer, or partner — every form of support directly changes lives in Mbale City.

Make a Donation

100% of your donation goes directly to programme delivery. We are committed to full transparency on how every dollar is spent.

🔒 Secure📋 Registered NGO✅ Transparent🌍 100% to Programmes

Your Impact Breakdown

Programme Delivery60%
Staff & Volunteers20%
Equipment & Materials12%
Admin & Compliance8%

Safeguarding commitment: HNG has a zero-tolerance policy on the misuse of donor funds. All transactions are independently audited annually and disclosed in our public Annual Report.

Volunteer With Us

Join our community of 100+ volunteers who give their time, skills, and passion to help children in Mbale City.

Open Opportunities

Sports Coach

Lead football, basketball, or athletics sessions at our community academies. 2–4 hrs/week.

Tutoring Volunteer

Support our after-school learning centres with literacy, numeracy, or STEM subjects.

Digital Skills Trainer

Teach basic computing, coding, or digital literacy in our tech hubs.

Fundraising Volunteer

Help us run events, campaigns, and donor outreach from wherever you are.

Safeguarding Support

Trained community volunteers who operate our child-friendly reporting network.

Communications & Design

Photography, videography, social media, or graphic design support.

Express Your Interest

Partner With Us

We work with international NGOs, corporate CSR programmes, government agencies, and foundations to co-design and co-fund initiatives that move the needle on child poverty in Eastern Uganda.

🏛️

Institutional Funders

Grant partnerships, multi-year funding agreements, and results-based financing models.

🏢

Corporate CSR

Employee volunteering, workplace giving, and branded programme sponsorships aligned to your ESG goals.

🤝

Technical Partners

Co-implement programmes, share expertise, or provide in-kind support (training, equipment, mentors).

📢

Media & Comms

Help us tell the story of Mbale City's children to audiences who can drive change.

Ready to explore a partnership?

Reach out to our Partnerships team. We'll respond within 3 working days with a tailored proposal.
